steps.title: Simplify the first equation
steps.content:
Add $200$ to both sides of the first equation.
$$x+33y-200=-359$$
$$x+33y=-159$$

steps.title: Match the line multiplier
steps.content:
For infinitely many solutions, the second equation must be a constant multiple of the first. The known coefficients show that multiplier is $\frac{1}{3}$.
$$\frac{11}{33}=\frac{-53}{-159}=\frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content:
The $x$-coefficient must use the same multiplier.
$$2w=\frac{1}{3}$$
$$w=\frac{1}{6}$$
The answer is 1/6.
